A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS MOOT. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RELIES UPON DEFENDANT'S ORIGINAL RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1. DEFENDANT AMENDED THAT RESPONSE AND THE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 15.1 SUPERSEDES THE ORIGINAL RESPONSE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS PRO PERFORMANCE SPORTS, LLC AND THE SPORTS AUTHORITY, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS BY WINNIE PANG. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT MAKES NO SHOWING OF THE RELEVANCE OF THE INQUIRY SUFFICIENT TO OVERCOME THE OBJECTIONS ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED. ORDER SIGNED. ATTY. CHRIS M. AMANTEA OF THE SQUIRE PATTON BOGGS, LLP, TEL. NO. (213)689-5118 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE COURT REGARDING LOADSTAR, HOURLY FEES WHICH IS QUITE HIGH, FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE PROPOSED ORDER AND PROPOSED JUDGMENT SUBMITTED TO THE COURT. PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint and for an approval of Proposition 65 Settlement - As part of the settlement, the parties stipulated that the complaint would be amended to include the DEHP and lead substantive allegation in the complaint. The court allowed the amended complaint to be deemed filed. The motion for approval of Proposition 65 Settlement is taken under submission. ORDER DEEMING FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED WAS SIGNED. Off Calendar Feb-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GREG SHEFFER, TEL. NO. 388-0911 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KATHRYN B. GRAY OF THE OGLETREE DEAKINS & STEWART LLP, TEL. NO. (858)652-3100 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PRO PERFORMANCE SPORTS LLC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Defendant The Sports Authority, Inc's Affirmative Defenses - The Court denies the motion without prejudice as moot. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication relies upon defendant's original response to form interrogatory 15.1. Defendant amended that response and the supplemental response to form interrogatory 15.1 supersedes the original response. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Answers To Deposition Questions And Production Of Documents By Winnie Pang
Off Calendar Jan-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PRO PERFORMANCE SPORTS, LLC AND THE SPORTS AUTHORITY, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO DEPOSITION QUESTIONS AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS BY WINNIE PANG

Appearances: Greg Sheffer of the Chanler Law Group (no phone number provided) for plaintiff and responding party; No other appearances.

There being no other appearances, all other parties are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne. Mr. Sheffer stipulates on behalf of plaintiff. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to compel answers to deposition questions and production of documents is denied. Defendant makes no showing of the relevance of the inquiry sufficient to overcome the objections asserted by plaintiff. No monetary sanctions will be imposed. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Not reported (302/JPT)
